module_mood_contract: practitioner fluency — 'I know how to test, I know how to learn, I know how to encode.' Not confidence-as-performance; competence-as-posture.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named and honoured per beat.
Mood doesn't resolve early — M6 close doesn't preempt M7.
Teaching moment lands reliably — 'one word' naming beat fires because the artifact is already in hand.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 12-19: the naming sequence (verifier → judge → gate → eval) arrives as recognition, not definition. Persona trace confirms: 'The word lands because the thing is already in hand.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity — file leads with discipline (naming → composing), not failure mode.
Module-to-module arc — M6 close hands off to M7 open.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 48: 'Agents that build agents. That is the flywheel. It starts with what you encoded today.' — opens appetite for M7 deliberation without resolving the arc.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; no authored conversations, pause cues, or room-share sentences.
Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lecture has no fenced prompts. No forcing functions to misplace.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block line 56 confirms: '15–18 min closing lecture for AE101 M6.' File is a naming/recognition lecture placed after exercises. Correct closer position.
This is a closing lecture, not an opener. Rule targets pre-exercise lecturing dosage.

Practitioner fluency is positive competence, not shame or having-done-wrong. 'Why the loop survives the model' section is epistemic reassurance (the stance survives model churn), not punitive correction.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity — does not front-run downstream teaching moments.
rules_evaluated.evidence: M6 names evals with full weight (strategy doc: 'M6 is where the word earns its full weight'). Scheduling callout is correctly a callout, not a mini-lecture. M7 deliberation not taught here — only forward-pointed. No front-running.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategic themes recur — compounding, eval-as-infrastructure, 90%-correct posture visible.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 14-18 name the eval primitive and composability (compounding theme). Lines 40-44 name Ronacher's three-pattern as stance (90%-correct / mechanically-self-aware theme). Lines 38-44 frame model-version anxiety resolution (encode-and-carry theme).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-exercise leap test — N/A for closing lectures.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Closing lecture, not an exercise. Leap test requirement applies to exercises.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch — N/A for closing lectures.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Closing lecture with no forcing functions. No phases to assign failure modes.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Inbound link pedagogical beat preserved — scheduling reference page link.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 36: link to 'Long-running shapes section in Claude Code for engineers' — the beat is 'scheduling primitives exist; here is where they live.' Bare-path link, correct form. The beat survives as an architecture callout, not a mini-lecture.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Student agency — no forced phases, no yoga-instructor pacing.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 36: 'You do not have to wire it today. You do need to know it exists' — explicitly frames scheduling callout as optional awareness, not compulsion.
